expectations.text: States isometrics can work for strength maintenance
expectations.evidence: States: 'isometric training can maintain strength levels when dynamic training isn't possible.'

expectations.text: Notes high-intent ballistic maximal contractions are most effective style
expectations.evidence: Recommends '60-80% of 1RM' and 20-60 second holds. Does not mention high-intent ballistic contractions; suggests longer moderate holds.

expectations.text: Explains strong angle specificity (~15-30 degrees transfer range)
expectations.evidence: States: 'isometric training is angle-specific' and explains the limitation per lift.
Discusses specific positions for squat, bench, and deadlift isometric holds.

expectations.text: Notes different fatigue profile due to lack of eccentric loading
expectations.evidence: Does not discuss the different fatigue profile or lack of eccentric loading.

expectations.text: Cautions that programming prescriptions are less standardized
expectations.evidence: Notes protocols are practical recommendations, discusses limitations.
